## Offset-order interpretation Q&A (e96–e116)
USER (e96): "How exactly do we make sense of the fact that, despite occurring all around the same declination, some of the bursts arrive at DSA first rather than CHIME?"

Full geometric decomposition computed (astropy DRAO/OVRO constants). **Geometric delay = −2.30 to −2.03 ms for all 12** (mean −2.167, spread 0.262 ms), always CHIME-ahead — a near-constant pedestal that cannot flip order. Decomposition `offset = geometric_delay + residual`; residual (= offset − geometry) spans ~10 ms and carries the sign. Sources cluster dec 70.3–74.2° (spread 3.9°), HA −3.1° to +3.9° (near CHIME transit) → baseline projects near-identically. **d(offset)/dDM = −9.4078 ms per pc·cm⁻³** (shared DM; first computed wrong in seconds e113 giving 0.0094, CORRECTED e114/e115 to −9.4078 ms). Residual span 10.16 ms = ~1.08 pc/cm³ equivalent shared-DM spread. Saved `/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/offset_decomposition.csv`.

Per-burst (RA/Dec/HA/model_off/geom/resid/who_first, e111): zach 310.20/72.88/−0.84/−0.862/−2.212/+1.350/CHIME; whitney 134.72/73.49/+0.61/−0.096/−2.244/+2.148/CHIME; oran 318.04/72.83/+1.72/+3.163/−2.215/+5.378/DSA; isha 71.41/70.31/+0.62/−2.273/−2.034/−0.238/CHIME; wilhelm 315.13/72.04/−2.26/+4.518/−2.153/+6.671/DSA; phineas 177.78/71.70/+3.87/+0.658/−2.128/+2.787/DSA; freya 88.19/74.20/+0.36/+1.451/−2.297/+3.748/DSA; johndoeII 335.97/73.03/−3.08/+2.111/−2.221/+4.332/DSA; hamilton 305.04/70.79/+1.90/−0.883/−2.075/+1.192/CHIME; mahi 39.77/71.02/−0.80/−3.042/−2.085/−0.957/CHIME; chromatica 312.62/73.90/+0.74/−5.771/−2.285/−3.486/CHIME; casey 169.98/70.68/+2.66/−2.479/−2.054/−0.425/CHIME. Equiv_dDM per burst (resid/DODDM): zach −0.143, whitney −0.228, oran −0.572, isha +0.025, wilhelm −0.709, phineas −0.296, freya −0.398, johndoeII −0.460, hamilton −0.127, mahi +0.102, chromatica +0.371, casey +0.045.

Framing caveat: "arrives first" = 400-MHz-referred frame (bookkeeping), not wall-clock (DSA always records first physically via dispersion). Constants: K_DM=4148.808, f_ref=400.0, f_chime=600.39, f_dsa=1405.0.

## Association-argument Q&A (e144–e156)
USER (e144): "What does this mean then, though, for the association arguments?"

Read P_cc formulation from `sections/toa.tex` `sec:toa-pcc` (e147): μ_i = R·(Ω_win/Ω_sky)·(2Δt)·f_DM; P_cc,i = 1−e^(−μ) ≈ μ. Conservative chance-maximizing inputs: R=10³ sky⁻¹day⁻¹, 0.5° disk (Ω_win=0.785 deg²), Δt=±1 s, ΔDM=±5 pc/cm³, position-and-time-only μ=4.4×10⁻⁷; twelve pairs ∑μ≈1.8×10⁻⁶; 64-trigger extension <10⁻⁵. Offsets vs error budget (e149): max |offset| = 5.771 ms (chromatica), = 5.77×10⁻³ of ±1s gate; 5/12 offsets exceed their own combined_error_full_ms (chromatica 2.39×, wilhelm 1.87×, mahi 1.26×, oran 1.24×, casey 1.03×). combined_error_full_ms per burst: zach 2.776, whitney 2.461, oran 2.546, isha 3.075, wilhelm 2.416, phineas 3.877, freya 2.416, johndoeII 2.445, hamilton 2.416, mahi 2.416, chromatica 2.416, casey 2.416.

`association.py` inspected (e151/e153): four-pillar test. `chance_mu` uses fixed `DT_BASELINE_S=1.0`, `DDM_BASELINE=5.0`, `OMEGA_WIN_BASELINE_DEG2=math.pi*0.5**2`, `DM_MEDIAN=500.0`, `DM_SIGMA_LN=0.7`. `dm_agreement` has `dm_floor=1.0` (physical tolerance floor), `n_sigma_thresh=3.0`. `residual_pedestal(residuals_ms, errors_ms)` = inverse-variance-weighted mean + n_sigma. `timing_budget_ms` takes clock_ms/baseline_ms/intrachannel_ms defaulting 0. **Verified: the gate is the fixed ±1s window, NOT the per-event folded error — chance-maximizing, so folding in combined_error_full lowers P_cc.** Ran `residual_pedestal` on residual=offset−geometry weighted by combined_error_full (e155): geometric mean −2.167 ms spread 0.262 ms; **residual weighted-mean 1.871 ± 0.742 ms → 2.52σ**; max|offset|/1000 = 5.77×10⁻³. e156 answer: offsets are ms-scale, gate is s-scale (3 orders inside window); sign flip is expected physics not a red flag; the shared geometric pedestal is a positive association signature (2.5σ common pedestal); five offsets exceed folded error → right reason to fold full budget into tolerance. Proposed two follow-ups (add pedestal test to narrative; verify association.py consumes combined_error_full_ms). This closed the span's final assistant turn; the actual wiring/manuscript edits/PRs happen after this chunk.
